| GRTS Cell Id: | <REQUIRED *either 'GRTS Cell Id' or 'Latitude' and 'Longitude' fields> Unique identifier of the 10x10 km NABat grid cell where the survey audio recording was captured. Mobile survey transects often span multiple NABat grid cells and can confuse data entry; it is HIGHLY RECOMMENDED this field be left empty if Latitude/Longitude values were logged during detector recording events.
Surveyor(s): <REQUIRED> Name of surveyor or data steward. For multiple entries; note that commas disrupt NABat database structure and should be omitted/replaced with other punctuation.
Latitude: <REQUIRED *either 'GRTS Cell Id' or 'Latitude' and 'Longitude' fields> Latitude in WGS84 decimal degrees. Mobile survey transects often span multiple NABat grid cells and can confuse data entry; it is HIGHLY RECOMMENDED the 'GRTS Cell Id' field is left empty and Latitude/Longitude values are provided when logged during detector recording events.
Longitude: <REQUIRED *either 'GRTS Cell Id' or 'Latitude' and 'Longitude' fields> Longitude in WGS84 decimal degrees. Mobile survey transects often span multiple NABat grid cells and can confuse data entry; it is HIGHLY RECOMMENDED the 'GRTS Cell Id' field is left empty and Latitude/Longitude values are provided when logged during detector recording events.
Site Name: <REQUIRED> A user-defined name for the specific mobile survey route. It is imperative to maintain consistent Site Names (i.e. exactly matching) year-to-year; do not append names with year or date values. Suggested naming convention: pair the starting point's 'GRTS Cell Id' with the term "Mobile" (e.g. 12345_Mobile).
Survey Start Time: <REQUIRED> Date and time that the survey recording began.
Survey End Time: <REQUIRED> Date and time that the survey recording concluded.
Unusual Occurrences: [RECOMMENDED] Indicate any unusual (non-weather) occurences from the recording period that could impact survey results. Examples include (but are not limited to): dead batteries; incorrect detection settings; filled data card; equipment damage; non-standard equipment setup; late start; etc. [150 character limit]
Significant Weather Event: [RECOMMENDED] Indicate whether one or more of the following weather events occured within the duration of the survey event: [A] temperature below 50F (10C); [B] precipitation (including fog) that exceeds 30 minutes or is intermittent throughout the survey event; [C] sustained wind speeds GREATER than 9 mph (4 m/s; Beaufort 3).
Auto Id Software: <REQUIRED> The brand and version of species autoclassifier software used to obtain the recording's 'Auto Id' value. If species labels were not auto-assigned; use 'no auto id' option.
Auto Id: <REQUIRED> The abbreviation code for the species identified by automated classification software. Both four- and six-digit codes are recognized options for single species. See ROW 2 of this field for options of all currently-accepted codes representing species/frequency groupings. If automated classification software was not employed when identifying calls to species; use "NoAutoId" option. [Note the present capitalization of species code options is for readability only; the 'Auto Id' field is NOT cAsE-sEnSiTiVe]
Manual Id: [RECOMMENDED] The abbreviation code for the species identified via manual inspection of its call waveform by a proficient individual. Both four- and six-digit codes are recognized options for single species. See ROW 2 of this field for options of all currently-accepted codes representing species/frequency groupings. [Note the present capitalization of species code options is for readability only; the 'Manual Id' field is NOT cAsE-sEnSiTiVe]
Manual Id Vetter: <REQUIRED *when 'Manual Id' is provided> The name of the proficient individual who visually reviewed the recorded waveform to confirm/reject/reassign the species identification listed in the 'Auto Id' field.
Name of Species List for Auto Id: <REQUIRED> Name of the user-created list for all species considered during the AUTO ID process. Users must create and name their species list(s) within the NABat Partner Portal project associated with these data. Legacy data with no specified species list should be submitted as 'legacy data unknown'.
Name of Species List for Manual Id: <REQUIRED *when 'Manual Id' is provided> Name of the user-created list for all species considered during the MANUAL VETTING process. The list of species considered during manual vetting may or may not match those considered during the auto-ID process. Should the assemblage of species considered during manual vetting differ from those considered during the auto-ID process; a separate species list must be defined within the associated NABat Partner Portal project. Legacy data with no specified species list should be submitted as 'legacy data unknown'.
Audio Recording Name: <REQUIRED> The file name of the audio recording. Include file extensions (e.g. *.wav *.zc ).
Audio Recording Time: <REQUIRED> The time at which the audio file was captured.
Microphone Orientation: The direction in which the microphone is positioned to detect sound; categorized relative to the direction of travel along the survey transect.
